title: Scope Contract
description: A declarative, operator-issued policy artifact defining the authorized operational scope of an autonomous AI agent. Fills the policy-artifact gap explicitly declared out-of-scope by IETF draft-klrc-aiagent-auth-00 §12. Evaluated by AuthZEN-compatible Policy Decision Points. License: MIT (SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT). Part of AgentMarshal — github.com/ApexForge13/agentmarshal.

scope_contract_version.description: Version of the Scope Contract specification this contract conforms to. Implementations MUST reject contracts with unrecognized versions.

contract_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this specific contract instance. RECOMMENDED format: UUIDv7 (time-ordered) or content hash (e.g., sha256:hex). Used as the immutable handle for audit-trail references and supersession chains.

tenant_id.description: Operator tenant namespace. REQUIRED in multi-tenant deployments; MAY be omitted in single-tenant deployments. Contracts are evaluated only within their tenant; cross-tenant evaluation MUST NOT occur. Recommended format: lowercase slug.

agent_id.description: Identifier of the agent this contract governs. SHOULD be a WIMSE/SPIFFE URI (e.g., spiffe://trust.example/agent/transaction-agent) when WIMSE infrastructure is present. MAY be a plain string identifier otherwise. Maps to AuthZEN subject.id at evaluation time.

issuer.description: Identity of the operator declaring this contract. Implementations SHOULD verify issuer authority before applying the contract.

not_before.description: Optional. Contract is not effective until this time. Defaults to issued_at if absent.

expires_at.description: Optional. Contract is no longer valid after this time. Implementations MUST deny all actions when the current time exceeds expires_at.

supersedes.description: Optional. contract_id of the prior contract this one replaces. The superseded contract becomes inactive at this contract's not_before. Walk this chain to reconstruct contract history.

version.description: Monotonically increasing version number within a supersession chain. The first contract in a chain has version 1.

declared_scope.description: Ordered list of authorization rules. First matching rule wins. If no rule matches an incoming request, the implicit default is DENY.

out_of_scope.description: Hard-deny list. Entries match action names or capability categories (or both, for unqualified string entries). Items here are denied regardless of declared_scope rules — a layered safety net that cannot be overridden by any rule. Implementations MUST evaluate out_of_scope BEFORE declared_scope. Two entry forms are supported: (a) unqualified string — matches action.name OR action.capability_category; (b) qualified object — matches only the specified field.

oneOf.description: Unqualified entry. Matches against the request's action.name OR action.capability_category. Use the qualified object form when precision is required.

escalation.description: Named escalation targets referenced by ScopeRule.decision.escalation_target. Required when any rule's decision.effect is 'escalate'.

bd_permissions.description: Optional ordered list of Bright Data (BD) call-authorization rules. Consulted ONLY by the AgentMarshal MCP proxy when an agent governed by this contract attempts a BD tool call; the declared_scope evaluator ignores this field. Semantics mirror declared_scope — first matching rule wins — but over a {service, tool, parameters} BD-call shape rather than an AuthZEN request. A rule fires when its 'match' predicate holds AND every named composite_check returns pass; the first such rule's 'permit' decision authorizes the call. No matching+passing rule ⇒ implicit deny. Absent ⇒ deny-all for BD calls (the agent is authorized for no BD service).

extensions.description: Reserved namespaces for protocol-specific or tenant-specific extensions. Implementations MUST validate known namespaces against their schemas and MAY ignore unknown namespaces.

^x-[a-z0-9_-]+$.description: Third-party extension namespaces. MUST use 'x-' prefix to avoid collision with reserved namespaces.

ScopeRule.description: A single authorization rule. The 'match' block describes predicates against an AuthZEN-shaped request (subject/action/resource/context). The 'decision' block describes the outcome when all predicates hold.

rule_id.description: Unique identifier for this rule within the contract. Used in audit records (matched_rule_id) and operator review surfaces.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this rule authorizes. Surfaced in operator review and dashboards.

composite_checks.description: Optional ordered list of composite predicate invocations. Each entry names a composite predicate registered via registerComposite() and supplies its input. All composites must be allowable (per isAllowable: pass-only; fail or stub blocks) for this rule's decision.effect to apply. A failed/stub composite causes the rule to be treated as non-matching, and the evaluator continues to the next rule. Omit composite_checks for pure match/decision rules.

CompositeCheck.description: A single composite-predicate invocation. The named composite must be registered in the dispatch registry at evaluation time; its evaluate() receives the supplied input. Input is intentionally untyped at the contract-schema layer — each composite predicate ships its own input schema (CompositePredicate.inputSchema) and the dispatcher validates against that before invoking evaluate().

predicate.description: Name of the composite predicate as registered via registerComposite(). Must match the composite's `name` field exactly.

input.description: Opaque input value passed to the composite's evaluate(). Schema is enforced by the composite's own inputSchema, not by the contract schema.

MatchPredicates.description: Predicates against an AuthZEN-shaped request. All four entity blocks (subject/action/resource/context) are independently optional. An omitted block matches anything. A present block must satisfy all of its predicates. At least one of the four blocks MUST be present to prevent unconstrained allow-everything rules. Predicate values are either literals (exact match) or constraint objects with operator keys.

EntityPredicate.description: Predicates against an AuthZEN entity. May include type/id/name predicates and a nested properties block. Property values may be literals (exact match) or constraint objects.

capability_category.description: Vendor-adapter capability category (e.g., 'vendor_purchase', 'outbound_sms', 'data_query'). Resolves against the vendor adapter registry, not against a hardcoded vendor list.

vendor_ref.description: Vendor reference predicate. Compact form 'capability_category:vendor_id' or expanded object. Resolution lives in the vendor adapter registry.

properties.description: Nested predicates over arbitrary AuthZEN entity properties. Keys are property names; values are literals or constraint objects.

Predicate.description: A constraint expression. Either a literal value (means exact-equals match) or an object with operator keys. Operator object MUST have at least one operator. By default, non-existence predicates (equals, in, pattern, min, max, etc.) IMPLICITLY require the property to be present in the request; use 'exists' to test presence explicitly without value constraints.

equals.description: Exact equality. Use 'equals: null' to match an explicitly-null property value.

in.description: Value must be in this set.

not_in.description: Value must not be in this set.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code; pairs with numeric min/max/between for monetary constraints. Currency mismatch between request and predicate causes the predicate to fail; no conversion is performed at evaluation time.

exists.description: True: property MUST be present and non-null in the request. False: property MUST be absent or null. Use to test presence/absence without value constraints.

not_exists.description: Inverse of exists. Provided for declarative readability; 'not_exists: true' is equivalent to 'exists: false'.

TimeWindowPredicate.description: Wall-clock time window predicate. Useful for compliance windows (TCPA outbound communication hours, business-hours-only operations).

Decision.description: Outcome when a rule's match predicates are all satisfied. Maps to AuthZEN response semantics: 'allow' -> decision:true; 'deny' -> decision:false; 'escalate' -> decision:false with step-up context populated.

escalation_target.description: Reference to a named target in escalation.targets. REQUIRED when effect = 'escalate'.

reason_code.description: Stable machine-readable code surfaced in audit records and AuthZEN response context. Stable across human-readable text changes.

reason.description: Human-readable reason. Surfaced in operator review UIs and (optionally) in AuthZEN response context.

EscalationConfig.description: Named escalation targets. Each target describes how to reach a human reviewer and what to do if review times out.

default_target.description: Name of the default target, used when a Decision specifies effect=escalate without an escalation_target.

address.description: Method-specific address. For sms: E.164 phone number. For email: RFC 5322 address. For webhook: HTTPS URL. For queue: queue identifier.

on_timeout.description: Behavior if the escalation target does not respond within timeout_seconds. Default DENY is fail-closed.

AP2Extension.description: AP2 (Agent Payments Protocol) interop extension. When a Scope Contract authorizes payment domains, it MAY reference AP2 Mandate issuers and declare aggregate caps across all transactions executed under such Mandates. Per-transaction cryptographic intent capture remains AP2's responsibility; the Scope Contract authorizes the agent to act under recognized Mandates up to declared aggregate limits.

accepted_mandate_issuers.description: DIDs or identifiers whose AP2 Mandates this contract recognizes as authorization grants.

aggregate_limit.description: Aggregate spend cap across all transactions executed under AP2 Mandates during the period.

WIMSEExtension.description: WIMSE/SPIFFE workload identity extension. When present, agent_id at the top level MUST be a valid SPIFFE URI, and additional WIMSE-specific verification requirements MAY be declared here.

required_attestation_evidence.description: Attestation evidence types required for the agent identity to be considered valid (e.g., 'tpm', 'aws-ec2-instance-identity').

credential_lifetime_max_seconds.description: Maximum acceptable lifetime of the agent's WIMSE credentials. Credentials older than this MUST be rejected.

BDPermissionRule.description: A single Bright Data call-authorization rule consulted by the MCP proxy. 'match' is a predicate over a {service, tool, parameters} BD-call shape; 'composite_checks' names composite predicates that must all pass; 'decision' is 'permit' when match holds and all composites pass. Note: composite_checks here is a plain string array of composite ids (the bd_call shape is threaded to each composite via action_properties at govern time) — deliberately simpler than declared_scope's CompositeCheck objects.

rule_id.description: Stable identifier for this rule within the contract. Surfaced in audit records (matched_rule_id) and operator review.

description.description: Human-readable purpose of this BD permission.

match.description: Predicate over the incoming BD call. 'service' is required; 'tool' and per-parameter predicates are optional further constraints.

service.description: Bright Data service this rule authorizes.

tool.description: Service-specific tool name (e.g. 'search_google' for serp_api). Omit to match any tool of the service.

parameters.description: Per-parameter predicates over the BD call parameters. Keys are parameter names; values are operator objects.

composite_checks.description: Names of composite predicates (registered via registerComposite) that must all return pass for this rule to fire. Plain string ids; input is the bd_call shape supplied via action_properties at govern time.

decision.description: Outcome when match holds and all composite_checks pass. Only 'permit' is meaningful; denial is implicit (no matching+passing rule).

BDParameterPredicate.description: A predicate over a single BD call parameter. Operator object with at least one operator. Distinct from the AuthZEN Predicate vocabulary: adds domain_in (URL-hostname match) and uses 'matches' for regex rather than 'pattern'.

in.description: Parameter value must be in this set.

exists.description: True: parameter MUST be present. False: MUST be absent.

matches.description: Regex (ECMA 262) the string parameter value must match. Use sparingly.

domain_in.description: URL parameter's hostname must match one of these domain patterns ('*.reuters.com' style).

BDConstraints.description: Optional per-rule operational constraints on the authorized BD calls. Bubble 17 records these in the contract; runtime enforcement of rate/cost ceilings lands in a later bubble.
